summary: Applied ADR 0250 from the latest origin/main candidate by replaying the monitoring stack on monitoring-lv3 under the documented immutable-guest exception, installing repo-managed Loki Canary assurance, surfacing its metrics and dashboard, moving canary-rule verification to alertmanager_runtime, and re-verifying Prometheus scrape health, Loki queryability, Alertmanager rule load, and Grafana access controls end to end.

verification.observed: `uv run --with pytest python -m pytest tests/test_monitoring_vm_role.py tests/test_alertmanager_runtime_role.py tests/test_validate_alert_rules.py -q` returned `13 passed in 0.91s`, `make syntax-check-monitoring` passed, and `UV_PROJECT_ENVIRONMENT=/tmp/proxmox-ws0250-validate ./scripts/validate_repo.sh alert-rules agent-standards yaml json role-argument-specs workstream-surfaces` completed successfully on the final integrated main worktree.

verification.check: Managed live replay from the main integration worktree
verification.observed: `BOOTSTRAP_KEY=/Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/proxmox_florin_server/.local/ssh/hetzner_llm_agents_ed25519 ALLOW_IN_PLACE_MUTATION=true make live-apply-service service=grafana env=production EXTRA_ARGS='-e bypass_promotion=true'` completed successfully from `/Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/proxmox_florin_server/.worktrees/ws-0250-main-integration-v2` with final recap `monitoring-lv3 : ok=191 changed=8 failed=0`. The replay used the documented ADR 0191 narrow in-place mutation exception because `monitoring-lv3` remains governed by immutable guest replacement.

Over the documented Proxmox jump path, `systemctl is-active loki-canary lv3-prometheus grafana-server` returned `active`, `systemctl list-units --type=service --all '*alertmanager*' --no-pager` showed `prometheus-alertmanager.service loaded active running`, and both `curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:9093/-/healthy` and `curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:9093/-/ready` returned `OK`.

verification.check: Loki Canary metrics and Prometheus scrape coverage
verification.observed: `curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:3500/metrics` returned `loki_canary_entries_total 18`, `loki_canary_metric_test_actual 13`, `loki_canary_metric_test_expected 13.170066666666667`, `loki_canary_missing_entries_total 0`, and `loki_canary_spot_check_missing_entries_total 0`. Prometheus returned one healthy scrape result for `up{job="loki-canary"}` with value `1` and labels including `service="loki-canary"`.

verification.check: Loki queryability and canary alert rules
verification.observed: Direct Loki queries against `query={name="loki-canary",stream="stdout"}` returned live canary entries with stream labels `name=loki-canary`, `stream=stdout`, and `service_name=loki-canary`. Prometheus rule inspection returned group `lv3-log-canary`, confirming the canary alert bundle was loaded after the rule-copy step in `alertmanager_runtime`.

verification.check: Grafana dashboard availability and public redirect
verification.observed: Guest-local Grafana API lookup with the managed admin secret returned dashboard title `LV3 Log Canary` for UID `lv3-log-canary-overview`, and `curl -k -I -sS https://grafana.lv3.org/d/lv3-log-canary-overview/lv3-log-canary-overview` returned `HTTP/2 302` with `location: /login?redirectTo=%2Fd%2Flv3-log-canary-overview%2Flv3-log-canary-overview`.

notes: The first live replay attempt exposed a verification-ordering issue because `monitoring_vm` checked for the canary rule group before `alertmanager_runtime` copied the platform alert rules. The fix moved that assertion into `collections/ansible_collections/lv3/platform/roles/alertmanager_runtime/tasks/verify.yml`.

The first end-to-end query verification also exposed that Loki Canary continued to persist its default labels, so the workstream removed the custom `-labels=` override and switched the verified selector to `{name="loki-canary",stream="stdout"}`.

The final repository validation run emitted the existing ADR 0163 top-level-directory warning before continuing, but the validator completed successfully with exit code 0 and the requested gates green.

This workstream became the final mainline integration step, so the protected release and canonical-truth files were updated after live verification: `VERSION`, `RELEASE.md`, `changelog.md`, `docs/release-notes/README.md`, `docs/release-notes/0.177.57.md`, `README.md`, `versions/stack.yaml`, `build/platform-manifest.json`, and the ADR plus workstream metadata.
